name: node-define-properties
title: node-define-properties
summary: Define multiple non-enumerable properties at once
description:
Define multiple non-enumerable properties at once
Uses Object.defineProperty when available; falls back to standard assignment
in older engines.
.
Existing properties are not overridden. Accepts a map of property names to a
predicate that, when true, force-overrides.
.
This include small packages:
- node-define-data-property, a data property shim on an object.
Will fall back to assignment in an engine without descriptors.
- node-es-define-property, Object.defineProperty compatibility layer
- globalthis, An ECMAScript spec-compliant polyfill/shim for
globalThis
.
Node.js is an event-based server-side JavaScript engine.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use node-define-properties because Define multiple non-enumerable properties at once.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use node-define-properties as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair node-define-properties with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `node-define-properties --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
